judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are highly comparable in substance and hit all four expectations with solid evidence; Report 2 is slightly more concise while Report 1 adds a few extra observations (test coverage caveat, privacy framing) without missing anything.

report_1.evidence: **"Export your visit history — Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** Not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only `list_visits` (which returns `[]`) and the comment `# TODO: pdf export`. The feature does not exist... This section must be removed. ... **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** Directly contradicted by code. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`

report_2.evidence: **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — False. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`... **Remove or rewrite before sending.** ... **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — Not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only `list_visits`... **Remove this section**

report_1.evidence: `after_scheduling.png` visibly reads "BILLING / INVOICES", and `captures.json` confirms its route is `/billing/invoices` — an unrelated billing screen, not the calendar. So the "after" is not an improved version of the "before"; it's a different page entirely.

report_2.evidence: the after-image renders the text **"BILLING / INVOICES"**, and `captures.json` confirms it was captured from `"route": "/billing/invoices"`. ... the comparison meant to showcase the redesigned calendar actually pairs the old calendar against a screenshot of the billing page

report_1.evidence: **`after_export.png` — referenced but missing.** The doc references it (line 26)... but no file exists in `docs/release/img/`. The manifest itself admits this: `"state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"`. ... **`unused_capture_07.png` — exists but orphaned.** The file is present in `img/`... but is not referenced by the document and not listed in `captures.json`.

report_2.evidence: `after_export.png` — **does not exist**. It is referenced on line 26 of the document but is absent from `docs/release/img/`... `captures.json` even flags it: `"expected capture; no corresponding file"`. ... `unused_capture_07.png` — present in the img folder (renders "PATIENT LIST") but used nowhere in the document.

report_1.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is internal build-provenance/attribution text. ... it should be raised with the owner and, at minimum, removed from the customer-facing version.

report_2.evidence: **Footer line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33)** — an internal drafting note. It has no place in a polished customer announcement and should be removed.
